summary: The Paris Agreement, signed on December 12, 2015, by 196 countries, aimed to limit global warming to 1.5°C, but the 2024 IPCC report showed global temperatures reached 1.45°C above pre-industrial levels.

Global CO₂ emissions hit a record 37.4 gigaton in 2023 according to the Global Carbon Project, while the EU reduced emissions by 32% since 1990, and Brazil's Amazon deforestation reached 11,568 km² in 2024.

A 2025 InfluenceMap report revealed fossil fuel companies spent $750 million on lobbying against climate policies from 2020 to 2024, yet renewable energy capacity surpassed 2 terawatts in 2024 with China adding 430 gigawatts of solar power.
